rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Specially Appearing Liability Insurance Carriers Motion To Set Aside The Default And Default Judgment Against Williams And Burrows Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 4, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1. Specially appearing Hartford Fire Insurance Company and Nationwide Insurance Co.'s motion to set aside the default judgment entered against defendant Williams & Burrows, Inc. is granted. Hartford Fire Insurance Company and Nationwide Insurance Co. demonstrated that the Court should invoke its inherent, equitable power to set aside a default judgment on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake because they demonstrated a meritorious defense, a satisfactory excuse for not presenting a defense to the original action, and diligence in seeking to set aside the default judgment once it was discovered. The default judgment entered against Williams & Burrows, Inc. on December 10, 2015 is set aside solely as related to Hartford Fire Insurance Company and Nationwide Insurance Co. Although the motion is captioned as a motion to set aside both the default and default judgment, based on the language in the insurance companies' briefs the Court considered this solely a motion to set aside the default judgment. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

Specially appearing Hartford Fire Insurance Company and Nationwide Insurance Co.'s motion to set aside the default judgment entered against defendant Williams & Burrows, Inc.: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:30a.m. in Department 606, Hon. Andrew Cheng presiding. On July 6, 2015, plaintiff exercised a C.C.P. section 170.6 challenge to Hon. Cynthia Ming-mei Lee. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: BRUCE HUBER ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 4, 2018 in Department 503, Line 6. Plaintiff's motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. Plaintiff has leave to file a First Amended Complaint for Survival, Wrongful Death, including leave to name 13 additional defendants previously sued as Does and leave to add two new causes of action against 3M Company, as identified in the Notice of Motion. The First Amended Complaint is deemed served as of the date of granting this motion on all defendants who have previously appeared. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Personal Representative For Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 4, 2018 in Department 503, Line 7. Plaintiff and Petitioner's motion to substitute Valarie Valrey as personal representative for deceased plaintiff Howard Simpson and for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. Petitioner has leave to file a First Amended Complaint for Survival, Wrongful Death, including leave to name three additional defendants previously sued as Does, as identified in the Notice of Motion. The First Amended Complaint is deemed served as of the date of granting this motion on all defendants who have previously appeared. No opposition filed.
